A JSTree version of my Perl.Wiki

CPAN::MetaCurator V 1.22. 2026-06-08T17:37:34

My home page, for downloading Perl.Wiki.html

Notes:
⊕ Nodes just under the root ('MetaCurator') are called Topics. Paragraphs is the TiddlyWiki equivalent.
⊕ Tokens like [[XS]] indicate a TiddlyWiki paragraph name, not a Perl module or topic.
⊕ Almost all leaf node are links. Use a middle-button click to open the link in another tab.
⊕ The code does not yet cleanly handle special cases such as the FAQ, pre-formatted paras.
⊕ Also, cases where the para is just a 'See also' are not yet clean.

⊕ My other module, CPAN::MetaPackages, builds & ships an SQLite db containing 02packages.details.txt.
⊕ Most recent version of 02packages.details.txt used: Last-Updated: Mon, 18 May 2026 22:29:02 GMT .
⊕ Acronyms: 0. Known modules: 264312. Leaves: 5434. Topics: 207. Unknown modules: 0

    • FAQ
      • https://www.schneier.com/crypto-gram/archives/2025/1115.html#cg4 - In training, we face poisoned datasets and backdoored models. In inference, we face adversarial inputs and prompt injection. During operation, we face a contaminated context and persistent compromise. We need semantic integrity: verifying not just data but interpretation, not just content but context, not just information but understanding. We can add checksums, signatures, and audit logs. But how do you checksum a thought? How do you sign semantics? How do you audit attention?.
      • https://www.schneier.com/crypto-gram/archives/2025/1115.html#cg4 - In training, we face poisoned datasets and backdoored models. In inference, we face adversarial inputs and prompt injection. During operation, we face a contaminated context and persistent compromise. We need semantic integrity: verifying not just data but interpretation, not just content but context, not just information but understanding. We can add checksums, signatures, and audit logs. But how do you checksum a thought? How do you sign semantics? How do you audit attention?.

We need semantic integrity: verifying not just data but interpretation, not just content but context, not just information but understanding. We can add checksums, signatures, and audit logs. But how do you checksum a thought? How do you sign semantics? How do you audit attention?.
      • https://www.schneier.com/crypto-gram/archives/2025/1115.html#cg4 - In training, we face poisoned datasets and backdoored models. In inference, we face adversarial inputs and prompt injection. During operation, we face a contaminated context and persistent compromise. We need semantic integrity: verifying not just data but interpretation, not just content but context, not just information but understanding. We can add checksums, signatures, and audit logs. But how do you checksum a thought? How do you sign semantics? How do you audit attention?.

    • OpenGL
      • Perl OpenGL (POGL) and Perl OpenGL::Modern (POGL2) development share common resources. At some point OpenGL::Modern will replace the legacy OpenGL implementation. Together they are referred to as POGL - Perl extension to Modern OpenGL API up to 4.5.
